message:
Add agent workflow runtime (#1)

* Add agent composition runtime

Add a serializable composition runtime on top of the existing
subprocess-backed agent execution path.

This keeps the standalone agent tool intact while introducing a new
compose tool, persistent composition lifecycle state, and a basic
composition UI surface.

The runtime supports spawn, sequence, fork, join, and loop flows,
plus budget checks and session reconstruction. It also adds tests for
composition behavior and updates existing tests for the refactor.

* Refine workflow run naming

Keep workflow as the public term for agent workflow definitions,
but use run as the user-facing term for live executions.

This renames the inspection command from /workflows to /runs,
updates the runtime UI copy, refreshes delegated context wording,
and aligns the README, changelog, and tests with the new naming.

* Split agent and run inspection commands

Make /agents and /runs list-only commands and add /agent and\n/run for inspecting a single item. This matches the command\nlayout users expect and gives clearer usage hints when list\ncommands receive an argument.\n\nUpdate the command tests and README so the documented and\nverified behavior stays in sync.

* Rename workflow compositions to runs

Replace the internal composition runtime terminology with run-based\nnames throughout the workflow engine, state, persistence layer,\nand public TypeScript types. This aligns the implementation with\nthe /runs and /run user-facing model.\n\nThis is a hard cut: remove composition aliases, composition event\nnames, and composition-specific environment variables. Update the\nREADME, changelog entry, and tests to match the new terminology.

* Serialize budget mutations through actor

Replace the shared mutable BudgetSnapshot with a BudgetActor that
serializes every read and mutation through a FIFO promise-chain
mailbox. Concurrent fork branches now safely share the same actor
without risking interleaved check-then-act operations.

The assertDepth + consumeChild pair becomes a single atomic
acquireSpawn message so no budget slot can be consumed between
the depth check and the child counter increment.

* Simplify README terminology

Remove the separate Flow Spec and Budgets concept sections in favor
of inline descriptions under Workflow and Runs. Replace references
to 'flow spec' with 'node tree' or 'workflow' throughout.

* Scope node counter per execute() call

Move the nodeCounter from the RunExecutor instance into a closure
inside execute() so concurrent or sequential workflow runs never
share ID state. The counter and its createNodeId closure are
threaded through EvaluationState.

Also remove the history-scanning fallback from resolveContinueValue
and document the resolution priority. The body result and its
summary are sufficient; probing the full reversed history could
silently match stale values from prior iterations.

* Remove unused join methods from AgentManager

The joinAll, joinAny, and joinQuorum methods were never called.
The executor implements its own fork/join logic directly.

* Improve /agents and /runs argument handling

When the user passes an argument to /agents or /runs, show a short
redirect hint instead of dumping the full list alongside an error.

* Document optional id fields for sequence and join

Add id field notes to the sequence and join sections so the
reference is consistent with spawn, fork, and loop.

* Add test for join agent reducer

Exercise the reducer: { kind: 'agent' } path in evaluateJoin,
which delegates fork summarization to a separate agent. Assert
that the reducer receives the branch results and its output
becomes the join result.

* Fix workflow run concurrency gaps

Make workflow cancellation and fork fail-fast behavior reliable.
Agent and workflow runs now share the same run runtime, direct
agent delegations appear in /runs, and run updates expose stable
snapshots instead of live mutable state.

Add regression coverage for fail-fast fork cancellation, the
post-abort spawn race, immutable snapshots, and direct agent run
persistence.

* Improve agent extension integration

Align the agent extension more closely with Pi core APIs.

Remove the defensive session_start guard, add typed event channel
constants, and provide custom tool call and result rendering for the
agent and workflow tools.

Also make unknown agents fail through the normal tool error path
instead of returning an ad-hoc isError field, and cover that behavior
in tests.

* Inherit parent model in delegated runs

Delegated agent and workflow spawns now fall back to the active
session model and thinking level when the agent definition omits
those fields.

This keeps the runtime behavior aligned with the tool output and
agent details, and avoids silently using the child process default
configuration instead of the caller's active session settings.

* Rebuild run state on session changes

The run widget and /runs commands now rebuild from the active
branch on session start, session switches, forks, and tree
navigation.

This keeps the extension aligned with pi's branching/session
abstractions instead of holding on to stale run state from a
previous branch or session.

* Honor delegated cwd for agent lookup

The agent tool now resolves project-local agents against the same
cwd that the delegated subprocess uses.

This prevents cross-project delegations from being validated in one
workspace and executed in another, which could reject valid agents
or pick the wrong project-local definition.

* Validate workflow ids and joins

Workflow validation now enforces unique node ids across the flow
and checks that every join references a fork node.

This prevents silent overwrites in execution memory and catches
invalid join wiring before any delegated agents start running.

* Bound workflow handoff context

Downstream workflow spawns now receive compact context previews
instead of the full nested outputs from every prior step.

This removes duplicated text, keeps named-node context bounded,
and truncates oversized payloads before they are appended to the
next delegated task prompt.

* Expose a typed workflow schema

The workflow tool now publishes a recursive TypeBox schema for
flow definitions instead of an untyped blob.

This gives tool callers structured guidance for spawn, sequence,
fork, join, and loop nodes before runtime validation runs.

* Import the shared thinking level type

Agent discovery now reuses the shared ThinkingLevel type instead
of redefining the same string union locally.

This keeps the frontmatter model aligned with pi's core agent
types and removes one source of drift in the extension.

* Deduplicate diagnostic formatting

Shared diagnostic text formatting now lives in one helper module
instead of being redefined in both the extension entrypoint and
the workflow executor.

This keeps agent discovery diagnostics consistent everywhere they
are rendered or passed into delegated runs.

* Extract tool schemas from the extension entry

The entrypoint now imports its tool schemas and extension-specific
error types from a dedicated module instead of defining them inline.

This trims down index.ts and separates tool metadata from runtime
wiring and execution logic.

* Extract presentation helpers from index

Rendering, formatting, and run-widget presentation logic now live
in a dedicated module instead of being mixed into the extension
entrypoint.

This leaves index.ts focused on wiring tools, commands, and runtime
behavior while presentation concerns stay isolated.

* Drop unused context from spawn engines

SpawnEngine now only receives the data it actually uses through
SpawnRequest instead of carrying an unused ExtensionContext
parameter through the subprocess path.

This tightens the engine abstraction and makes it clearer which
inputs delegated spawn implementations depend on.

* Clean up executor readability and drop unused dep

- Simplify resolveContinueValue to a two-step lookup (output.<path>
  then raw <path>), remove unused memory parameter and stale comment.
- Rename primaryFailure() to getPrimaryFailure() so the method no
  longer shadows the state field name.
- Remove no-op catch block in evaluateSpawn that only re-threw.
- Drop unused DelegatedAgentRunError import left behind by the
  catch removal.
- Remove phantom @mariozechner/pi-agent-core dev dependency that
  was never imported.

* Fix workflow join semantics

Short-circuit fork execution when joins use any or quorum so\nworkflows can proceed as soon as the success condition is met.\n\nReject joins that target future or out-of-scope forks and disallow\nmultiple joins for the same fork to keep execution behavior\nunambiguous.

* Fix TUI line wrapping in agent renderer

Wrap custom agent and workflow render output to the terminal
width so long task and result text no longer crashes the TUI.

Add regression tests that assert rendered lines stay within
the requested width for narrow terminals.

* Fix duplicate run spinners in TUI

Keep the global Working spinner as the only animated indicator.
Render the runs widget with static status markers and collapse the
redundant child row for single spawn runs that repeat the run label.

Also add a regression test for the simplified runs widget output.

* Add deterministic Mermaid diagram export

Add toMermaid(flow, options?) that converts a FlowSpec into a Mermaid
flowchart string. The same input always produces the same output:
node IDs are counter-based (n0, n1, …), fork branches are visited
in sorted key order, and class definitions follow a fixed order.

Node shapes encode the flow kind: spawn as stadium, fork and join as
diamonds, and loop as hexagon. Color-coded classDef styles distinguish
the node types visually.

* Add flow tree visualization and /flow command

Add formatFlowTree() that renders a FlowSpec as an ASCII tree with
kind icons: ● spawn, ◇ fork, ◆ join, ◎ loop. Sequences are
transparent — their children inline at the parent indentation level.
When runtime state is available, status icons replace kind icons:
✔ completed, ✘ failed, ⠹ running, ◌ waiting, ■ aborted, ○ queued.

Register /flow [id] command for inspecting a run's structure as an
ASCII tree, with /flow [id] mermaid for Mermaid export. Enhance
renderWorkflowCall to show the flow tree inline when the workflow
tool is invoked.

Replace the static string-array widget with a timer-driven
RunWidgetManager that cycles braille spinner frames at 80 ms,
following the pi-subagents pattern. Single-spawn runs show only
the header line to avoid redundancy. Multi-node workflows show the
full flow tree with animated status overlay.

* Skip duplicate root label in widget flow tree

When the flow tree's root node label matches the run header, omit
it to avoid showing the same name twice.

* Resolve status icons for nodes without spec IDs

Extend the node index to also look up RunNodes by label and branch
key. This lets flowIcon() resolve status for spawn nodes that the
LLM created without an explicit id field, so fork branches show
animated spinners instead of static kind icons.

* Replace footer status with run counts event

Remove the setStatus call from the widget and emit a
RUN_COUNTS_CHANGED event with {runs, running, waiting, queued}
on every state change. Other extensions can listen to this event
and display the counters wherever they see fit.

* Improve workflow authoring and validation

Add compact workflow syntax, normalize agent references, and
inject a discovered workflow agent catalog into orchestration
prompts. The runtime now preflights workflow agent references
before spawning children, which cuts retry churn and makes
workflow authoring more reliable.

* Polish workflow rendering

Render compact summaries for structured workflow outputs,
hide transient result text while runs are still active, and
use canonical spec paths to overlay branch and loop status
reliably in the flow tree.

* Wrap agent and workflow results in XML envelopes

Return tool results in structured XML so the orchestrating LLM
can parse multi-branch outputs more reliably. Fork results use
<branch> and <error> elements; simple outputs get a plain wrapper.

TUI renderers strip the envelope before display so users see
clean text.

* Fix XML result envelopes

Escape agent and workflow result payloads before embedding them in XML envelopes so delegated output cannot break the wrapper structure.

Serialize workflow outputs losslessly inside explicit <output> elements instead of reinterpreting arbitrary JSON objects based on their shape. This preserves user payloads such as objects with branches or errors fields.

Add regression tests for XML-looking payloads and lossless workflow JSON round-tripping.

* Add detachable workflow runs

Let Escape detach already-started agent and workflow runs so they\ncontinue in the background instead of being terminated with the\nforeground operation.\n\nAdd explicit lifecycle control through /runs and /run stop, keep\ndetached runs persisted against their origin session, and surface the\ndetached state in run details.\n\nHide detached runs from the live widget so the TUI only shows\nforeground-attached work while background runs remain available through\nslash commands.

* Unify flow inspection and watch mode

Make flow the public execution surface instead of the old\nrun split. Add /flows for browsing, turn /flow into the\nprimary inspect command, and support omitted IDs through an\ninteractive picker in TUI mode.\n\nAdd /flow watch for re-entering live execution with an\nanimated tree, explicit stop handling, and detach semantics\nthat match the foreground/background UX. Update user-facing\ncopy, docs, and tests to match the new flow model.

* Add detached workflow notifications

* Use status icons in workflow notifications

* Refine flow inspection presentation

* Show static flow structure in inspect view

The inspect output now shows the flow structure using static kind
icons instead of overlaying runtime status. This makes the structure
section a stable reference of what the flow looks like, independent
of how far execution has progressed.

Runtime status is still visible in the Nodes summary line and the
Latest node field.

* Add expand/collapse hints to run notifications

Collapsed notifications now truncate long summaries and errors to
four lines and show a key hint for expanding. Expanded notifications
show a hint for collapsing. Notifications without meaningful extra
detail skip the hint entirely.

* Skip last-step summary in final sequence notification

For multi-step sequences the final run notification no longer
repeats the last step's output as its summary. Each step already
posts its own notification, so duplicating the last one in the
final message added noise without new information.

* Add Unicode flow diagram via beautiful-mermaid

Adds renderFlowAscii() which converts a FlowSpec to Mermaid text
and renders it as Unicode box-drawing art using beautiful-mermaid.
Supports a plain ASCII fallback and configurable ANSI color modes.

The diagram is available through /flow diagram <id>, the d shortcut
in the flow picker, and the Diagram option in the /flows menu.

* Connect root sequence siblings visually

Root-level sequences with multiple steps were rendered without tree
connectors, making joins appear disconnected from their forks. Now
multi-step root sequences use the same connector lines as nested
sequences, tying siblings together with │ continuation lines.

* Remove redundant arrows from flow tree

Drop → in fork branches and ← in join labels. The tree structure
already conveys which agent handles a branch and which fork a join
collects from. Branch lines now use a colon separator instead.

* Let icons carry node types in flow trees

Drop the redundant 'join:' prefix from join labels since ◆ already
signals the type. Swap branch lines to show the agent first and
branch key second, matching the consistent pattern where the icon
conveys the node type and the text identifies the instance.

* Color-code flow tree icons and dim connectors

Status icons get semantic colors (success, error, accent, warning,
dim) and kind icons get accent coloring to stand out from the tree
chrome. Tree-drawing characters (├─ └─ │) are dimmed to recede
visually. Coloring is applied only when a theme is available;
plain-text callers like formatFlowInspectText are unaffected.

* Add node result summaries to flow inspect

* Show live flow results while watching

Surface node outputs directly in /flow watch so users can follow
agent results without detaching first.

Also extract reusable node result formatting for inspect and watch
views, add presentation coverage, and keep the full result history
visible while a flow is still running.

* Restore expand hints for previewed output

Show expand and collapse hints again for truncated preview sections
in agent and workflow renderers.

Also render the 'more lines' suffix in dim styling so previewed
output matches the usual visual treatment and is less confusing.

* Fix changelog example and add claude as co-author

Sync the review-loop example with the README: use typed field
annotations in the reviewer task prompt and add explicit output:text
to the engineer spawn. Also add claude to the authors list.

files.patch:
@@ -9,18 +9,57 @@ A generic framework for agent orchestration in
 pi install npm:pi-agents
 ```
 
-Where agents are loaded from:
+Agents are loaded from:
 
 - **User agents:** `~/.pi/agents/*.md`
-- **Project agents:** nearest `.pi/agents/*.md` (searched upward from your current working directory)
+- **Project agents:** the nearest `.pi/agents/*.md`, searched upward from your
+  current working directory
 
-The tool defaults to **both** (project + user) agents.
+The tools default to **both** project and user agents.
+
+## 📖 Concepts
+
+The following concepts form the building blocks of the framework: **agents**,
+**workflows**, and **runs**.
+
+### Agent
+
+An **agent** is a markdown file that defines a delegated pi subprocess. Each
+file has YAML frontmatter (name, model, thinking level, skills) and a body that
+becomes the agent's system prompt. At runtime the framework launches the agent
+as an isolated pi process, waits for it to finish, and returns the result.
+
+### Workflow
+
+A **workflow** is a JSON-defined graph that orchestrates multiple agents. You
+pass the graph to the `workflow` tool, and the runtime walks it node by node.
+A workflow is defined as a tree of **nodes**, where each node has a `kind` that
+determines its behavior:
+
+| Node       | Purpose                                                         |
+| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `spawn`    | Run a single agent as a subprocess and return its output.       |
+| `sequence` | Run a list of nodes one after another, threading results.       |
+| `fork`     | Run named branches concurrently (up to a concurrency limit).    |
+| `join`     | Wait for the branches of a previous `fork` and combine results. |
+| `loop`     | Repeat a body node until a condition is met or a cap is hit.    |
+
+Nodes nest recursively: a `sequence` can contain `fork` nodes, a `loop`
+body can be a `sequence`, and so on. You can optionally set **budgets** to
+constrain execution (max depth, max parallelism, max iterations, etc.).
+
+### Flows
+
+Every agent delegation and workflow execution is persisted as a **flow** in the
+current pi session. Flows survive session reloads so you can inspect past
+results. Use `/flows` to browse them and `/flow <id>` to inspect one in
+detail.
 
 ## 🚀 Quick start
 
 ### 1. Create an agent file
 
-For example `.pi/agents/explorer.md` in your project:
+For example, create `.pi/agents/explorer.md` in your project:
 
 ```md
 ---
@@ -43,24 +82,349 @@ Return a compact handoff with concrete file paths.
 
 Everything below the frontmatter is the agent's system prompt.
 
-### 2. Start Pi
+### 2. Start pi
 
 ```sh
 pi
 ```
 
-This repo already includes project-local examples in `.pi/agents/` (`explorer`, `worker`).
+This repo already includes project-local examples in `.pi/agents/`
+(`explorer`, `worker`).
 
-### 3. Spawn an agent
+### 3. Delegate a single agent
 
 Ask naturally:
 
 - `Use the explorer agent to find where auth is implemented.`
 
-### 4. Inspect agent definitions
+You can also call the `agent` tool directly:
+
+```json
+{
+  "name": "explorer",
+  "task": "Find where auth is implemented.",
+  "scope": "both"
+}
+```
+
+### 4. Run a workflow
+
+Use the `workflow` tool to run a multi-agent workflow. The tool accepts both
+the full canonical JSON tree and a compact authoring form for common cases.
+The example below uses the canonical form and defines a review loop: a
+`reviewer` agent inspects the patch, then an `engineer` agent applies the
+findings, repeating until the reviewer signals `done` or three iterations have
+passed.
+
+```json
+{
+  "label": "review loop",
+  "flow": {
+    "kind": "loop",
+    "id": "review-loop",
+    "maxIterations": 3,
+    "continueWhen": {
+      "kind": "result_field",
+      "path": "done",
+      "equals": false
+    },
+    "body": {
+      "kind": "sequence",
+      "steps": [
+        {
+          "kind": "spawn",
+          "id": "review",
+          "agent": "reviewer",
+          "task": "Review the current patch. Return JSON with done:boolean, findings:string[], and summary:string.",
+          "output": "json"
+        },
+        {
+          "kind": "spawn",
+          "id": "implement",
+          "agent": "engineer",
+          "task": "Implement the latest review findings.",
+          "output": "text"
+        }
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "budgets": {
+    "maxIterations": 3,
+    "maxParallelism": 2
+  }
+}
+```
+
+The runtime launches each `spawn` as a subprocess and persists lifecycle events
+into the session so pi can reconstruct state after a reload.
+
+## 🔧 Available tools
+
+### `agent`
+
+Runs one isolated delegated agent as a subprocess.
+
+Parameters:
+
+- `name`: Agent name from markdown frontmatter.
+- `task`: The delegated task.
+- `scope`: Optional. One of `user`, `project`, or `both`.
+- `cwd`: Optional working directory for the delegated process.
+
+### `workflow`
+
+Runs a workflow defined by a JSON tree of nodes.
+
+The tool also accepts a compact authoring form:
+
+- A plain spawn can omit `kind: "spawn"`.
+- A `fork` can provide default `agent`, `taskTemplate`, `cwd`, `scope`, and
+  `output` values for its branches.
+- Fork branch values can be full flow specs, spawn-shorthand objects, or plain
+  agent-name strings.
+
+For example:
+
+```json
+{
+  "label": "Four-Lens Code Review",
+  "flow": {
+    "kind": "fork",
+    "id": "review-fork",
+    "agent": "reviewer",
+    "taskTemplate": "Review this codebase from the {branch} lens.",
+    "branches": {
+      "architecture": {},
+      "readability": "reviewer",
+      "tests": { "task": "Review test coverage and test quality." },
+      "ux": { "agent": "ux-reviewer" }
+    }
+  }
+}
+```
+
+The runtime normalizes this compact form into the full canonical workflow
+before validation and execution.
+
+Top-level parameters:
+
+- `label`: Optional human-readable label for this workflow run.
+- `flow`: The workflow definition (a JSON tree of nodes).
+- `budgets`: Optional limits—`maxDepth`, `maxChildren`, `maxParallelism`,
+  `maxIterations`.
+- `scope`: Optional default agent scope for all `spawn` nodes.
+- `cwd`: Optional default working directory for all `spawn` nodes.
+
+## 🧭 Commands
+
+| Command                        | Description                                |
+| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------ |
+| `/agents`                      | List discovered agents.                    |
+| `/agent <name>`                | Show full details for one agent.           |
+| `/flows`                       | Browse recorded flows in the current session.      |
+| `/flow`                        | Open the interactive flow picker.                  |
+| `/flow <id-or-prefix>`         | Inspect one flow.                                  |
+| `/flow watch [id-or-prefix]`   | Re-enter live watch mode for a running flow.       |
+| `/flow mermaid [id-or-prefix]` | Output the selected flow as a Mermaid diagram.     |
+| `/flow stop [id-or-prefix]`    | Stop a running flow.                               |
+
+### Flow inspect view
+
+`/flow <id>` shows the selected flow's metadata, current status, and an ASCII
+tree of its structure. Each node kind has a distinct icon:
+
+| Icon | Kind  |
+| ---- | ----- |
+| `●`  | spawn |
+| `◇`  | fork  |
+| `◆`  | join  |
+| `◎`  | loop  |
+
+Sequences are transparent — their children appear at the parent indentation
+level without extra nesting.
+
+When you inspect a completed or running flow, the kind icons are replaced by
+status icons:
+
+| Icon | Status    |
+| ---- | --------- |
+| `✔`  | completed |
+| `✘`  | failed    |
+| `⠹`  | running   |
+| `◌`  | waiting   |
+| `■`  | aborted   |
+| `○`  | queued    |
+
+Example — static flow tree:
+
+```
+● initializer
+◇ parallel
+├─ fast → ● fast-worker
+└─ slow
+   ├─ ● prep
+   └─ ● slow-worker
+◆ join: all ← parallel
+◎ validate (max 3)
+└─ ● validator
+```
+
+Example — with status overlay:
+
+```
+✔ initializer
+⠹ parallel
+├─ fast → ✔ fast-worker
+└─ slow
+   ├─ ✔ prep
+   └─ ⠹ slow-worker
+◌ join: all ← parallel
+○ validate (max 3)
+└─ ○ validator
+```
+
+In interactive mode, omitting the flow ID opens a picker. The picker supports
+single-key actions on the selected flow:
+
+- `Enter` inspect
+- `w` watch
+- `m` mermaid
+- `s` stop
+
+The `watch` action is only available for running flows. Once watching, `Esc`
+detaches back to the normal UI and `s` stops the flow.
+
+### Mermaid export
+
+Use `mermaid` to get a Mermaid flowchart you can paste into GitHub, docs,
+or [mermaid.live](https://mermaid.live):
+
+```
+/flow mermaid 3a8bc2f1
+```
+
+The output is deterministic — the same workflow always produces the same
+diagram. Node IDs are counter-based (`n0`, `n1`, …), fork branches are visited
+in sorted key order, and there are no random elements.
+
+The flow tree also appears inline when the `workflow` tool is invoked, giving
+you a structural preview of what is about to run. The live widget in the status
+bar overlays status icons so you can track progress at a glance.
+
+## 🗂️ Node reference
+
+### `spawn`
+
+Run a single agent as a subprocess. This is the leaf node of every workflow—the
+only node kind that actually executes work.
+
+```json
+{
+  "kind": "spawn",
+  "id": "review",
+  "agent": "reviewer",
+  "task": "Review the current patch.",
+  "scope": "both",
+  "cwd": "/path/to/project",
+  "output": "json"
+}
+```
+
+- `id`: Optional identifier, used to reference this node's result elsewhere.
+- `agent`: Name of the agent (must match a discovered agent's frontmatter).
+- `task`: The task prompt sent to the agent.
+- `output`: `"text"` (default) or `"json"` (the agent's output is parsed as
+  JSON, useful for downstream `continueWhen` checks).
+
+### `sequence`
+
+Run a list of nodes one after another. The output of the sequence is the output
+of its last step.
+
+```json
+{
+  "kind": "sequence",
+  "steps": [
+    { "kind": "spawn", "agent": "reviewer", "task": "Review." },
+    { "kind": "spawn", "agent": "engineer", "task": "Implement." }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+- `id`: Optional. When set, downstream nodes can reference this sequence's
+  result by name.
+
+### `fork`
+
+Run named branches concurrently. Each branch is an arbitrary node tree. Use
+`concurrency` to cap how many branches run in parallel.
+
+```json
+{
+  "kind": "fork",
+  "id": "fanout",
+  "branches": {
+    "a": { "kind": "spawn", "agent": "reviewer", "task": "Review for bugs." },
+    "b": { "kind": "spawn", "agent": "reviewer", "task": "Review for style." }
+  },
+  "concurrency": 2
+}
+```
+
+- `id`: Required. Referenced by a downstream `join` node.
+- `branches`: A map of branch keys to node trees.
+- `concurrency`: Optional cap on simultaneous branches.
+
+### `join`
+
+Wait for the branches of a previous `fork` and combine their results.
+
+```json
+{
+  "kind": "join",
+  "from": "fanout",
+  "mode": "all",
+  "reducer": { "kind": "collect" },
+  "onFailure": "collectErrors"
+}
+```
+
+- `id`: Optional. When set, downstream nodes can reference this join's result.
+- `from`: The `id` of the `fork` node to join.
+- `mode`: When to proceed—`"all"` (every branch must finish), `"any"` (first
+  success wins), or `"quorum"` (a minimum number of successes, set by the
+  `quorum` field).
+- `reducer`: How to combine branch results. `"collect"` gathers them into an
+  object keyed by branch name. `"agent"` delegates summarization to another
+  agent.
+- `onFailure`: `"failFast"` (default, abort on first branch error) or
+  `"collectErrors"` (continue and gather errors alongside successes).
+
+### `loop`
+
+Repeat a body node until a condition is met or `maxIterations` is reached.
+
+```json
+{
+  "kind": "loop",
+  "id": "review-loop",
+  "body": { "kind": "spawn", "agent": "reviewer", "task": "Review." },
+  "maxIterations": 3,
+  "continueWhen": {
+    "kind": "result_field",
+    "path": "done",
+    "equals": false
+  }
+}
+```
 
-- `/agents` — list discovered agents
-- `/agents <name>` — show full details for one agent
+- `id`: Required.
+- `body`: Any node tree to execute each iteration.
+- `maxIterations`: Hard cap on repetitions.
+- `continueWhen`: Optional predicate evaluated after each iteration. Currently
+  supports `result_field`, which checks a single field in the body's JSON
+  output. The loop continues while the field matches `equals` and stops
+  otherwise.
 
 ## 📄 License

@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+---
+title: Add agent workflow runtime
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - codex
+  - claude
+created: 2026-03-15T00:00:00Z
+---
+
+You can now orchestrate multiple agents with the new `workflow` tool. Define a
+flow spec as a JSON tree of nodes, and the runtime executes them for you—
+running each agent as an isolated subprocess.
+
+Five node kinds are available:
+
+- **`spawn`**: run a single agent and return its output as text or parsed JSON.
+- **`sequence`**: run steps one after another, threading results forward.
+- **`fork`**: run named branches concurrently, with an optional concurrency cap.
+- **`join`**: wait for a fork's branches to finish, combining results via a
+  `collect` or `agent` reducer. Supports `all`, `any`, and `quorum` modes.
+- **`loop`**: repeat a body node up to `maxIterations` times, with an optional
+  `continueWhen` predicate that checks a field in the body's JSON output.
+
+Budgets let you cap nesting depth, child count, parallelism, and loop
+iterations across the entire workflow.
+
+For example, a review loop that alternates between a reviewer and an engineer
+until the reviewer signals `done` (or three iterations pass):
+
+```json
+{
+  "label": "review loop",
+  "flow": {
+    "kind": "loop",
+    "id": "review-loop",
+    "maxIterations": 3,
+    "continueWhen": {
+      "kind": "result_field",
+      "path": "done",
+      "equals": false
+    },
+    "body": {
+      "kind": "sequence",
+      "steps": [
+        {
+          "kind": "spawn",
+          "id": "review",
+          "agent": "reviewer",
+          "task": "Review the current patch. Return JSON with done:boolean, findings:string[], and summary:string.",
+          "output": "json"
+        },
+        {
+          "kind": "spawn",
+          "id": "implement",
+          "agent": "engineer",
+          "task": "Implement the latest review findings.",
+          "output": "text"
+        }
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "budgets": { "maxIterations": 3, "maxParallelism": 2 }
+}
+```
+
+Every workflow execution is persisted as a flow in your session. Use `/flows`
+to browse past flows and `/flow <id>` to inspect one in detail. Flows survive
+session reloads.

@@ -0,0 +1,170 @@
+import { StringEnum } from "@mariozechner/pi-ai";
+import { Type } from "@sinclair/typebox";
+import type { AgentRunDetails } from "./types.js";
+
+export type ToolPromptMetadata = {
+  promptSnippet?: string;
+  promptGuidelines?: string[];
+};
+
+export class UnknownAgentError extends Error {
+  readonly details: AgentRunDetails;
+
+  constructor(message: string, details: AgentRunDetails) {
+    super(message);
+    this.name = "UnknownAgentError";
+    this.details = details;
+  }
+}
+
+const ScopeSchema = StringEnum(["user", "project", "both"] as const, {
+  description:
+    'Which agents to load. "user" reads ~/.pi/agents. "project" reads nearest .pi/agents. "both" merges both (project wins).',
+  default: "both",
+});
+
+export const AgentParamsSchema = Type.Object({
+  name: Type.String({
+    description: "Name of the agent definition from markdown frontmatter",
+  }),
+  task: Type.String({ description: "Task to delegate" }),
+  scope: Type.Optional(ScopeSchema),
+  cwd: Type.Optional(
+    Type.String({
+      description: "Working directory for the delegated agent process",
+    }),
+  ),
+});
+
+const PositiveIntegerSchema = Type.Integer({ minimum: 1 });
+const OutputModeSchema = StringEnum(["text", "json"] as const);
+const JoinModeSchema = StringEnum(["all", "any", "quorum"] as const);
+const JoinFailureSchema = StringEnum(["failFast", "collectErrors"] as const);
+
+const ContinueSpecSchema = Type.Object({
+  kind: Type.Literal("result_field"),
+  path: Type.String({
+    description: "Field path to inspect on the body result",
+  }),
+  equals: Type.Boolean({
+    description: "Loop while the field equals this value",
+  }),
+});
+
+const JoinReducerSchema = Type.Union([
+  Type.Object({ kind: Type.Literal("collect") }),
+  Type.Object({
+    kind: Type.Literal("agent"),
+    agent: Type.String({ description: "Agent used to reduce branch results" }),
+    task: Type.String({ description: "Reducer task prompt" }),
+    output: Type.Optional(OutputModeSchema),
+  }),
+]);
+
+const SpawnShorthandSchema = Type.Object({
+  id: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+  label: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+  agent: Type.Optional(Type.String({ description: "Agent name to execute" })),
+  task: Type.Optional(
+    Type.String({ description: "Task prompt passed to the agent" }),
+  ),
+  cwd: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+  scope: Type.Optional(ScopeSchema),
+  output: Type.Optional(OutputModeSchema),
+});
+
+const FlowSpecSchema = Type.Recursive((Self) =>
+  Type.Union([
+    Type.Object({
+      kind: Type.Literal("spawn"),
+      id: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      label: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      agent: Type.String({ description: "Agent name to execute" }),
+      task: Type.String({ description: "Task prompt passed to the agent" }),
+      cwd: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      scope: Type.Optional(ScopeSchema),
+      output: Type.Optional(OutputModeSchema),
+    }),
+    SpawnShorthandSchema,
+    Type.Object({
+      kind: Type.Literal("sequence"),
+      id: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      label: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      steps: Type.Array(Self, {
+        description:
+          "Nodes executed in order; the last output becomes the sequence output",
+      }),
+    }),
+    Type.Object({
+      kind: Type.Literal("fork"),
+      id: Type.Optional(
+        Type.String({
+          description: "Optional fork identifier for downstream joins",
+        }),
+      ),
+      label: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      agent: Type.Optional(
+        Type.String({
+          description: "Default agent inherited by branch spawns",
+        }),
+      ),
+      taskTemplate: Type.Optional(
+        Type.String({
+          description:
+            "Default task template for branch spawns. Use {branch} as a placeholder.",
+        }),
+      ),
+      cwd: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      scope: Type.Optional(ScopeSchema),
+      output: Type.Optional(OutputModeSchema),
+      branches: Type.Record(
+        Type.String(),
+        Type.Union([Self, Type.String(), SpawnShorthandSchema]),
+        {
+          description:
+            "Named branches executed concurrently. Branch values may be full flow specs, spawn shorthands, or agent-name strings.",
+        },
+      ),
+      concurrency: Type.Optional(PositiveIntegerSchema),
+    }),
+    Type.Object({
+      kind: Type.Literal("join"),
+      id: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      label: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      from: Type.String({ description: "Fork id to join" }),
+      mode: JoinModeSchema,
+      quorum: Type.Optional(PositiveIntegerSchema),
+      reducer: Type.Optional(JoinReducerSchema),
+      onFailure: Type.Optional(JoinFailureSchema),
+    }),
+    Type.Object({
+      kind: Type.Literal("loop"),
+      id: Type.String({ description: "Unique loop identifier" }),
+      label: Type.Optional(Type.String()),
+      body: Self,
+      maxIterations: PositiveIntegerSchema,
+      continueWhen: Type.Optional(ContinueSpecSchema),
+    }),
+  ]),
+);
+
+export const WorkflowParamsSchema = Type.Object({
+  label: Type.Optional(
+    Type.String({ description: "Optional label shown in workflow UI" }),
+  ),
+  flow: FlowSpecSchema,
+  budgets: Type.Optional(
+    Type.Object({
+      maxDepth: Type.Optional(PositiveIntegerSchema),
+      maxChildren: Type.Optional(PositiveIntegerSchema),
+      maxParallelism: Type.Optional(PositiveIntegerSchema),
+      maxIterations: Type.Optional(PositiveIntegerSchema),
+    }),
+  ),
+  scope: Type.Optional(ScopeSchema),
+  cwd: Type.Optional(
+    Type.String({
+      description: "Working directory for relative agent discovery and tasks",
+    }),
+  ),
+});
